agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical SQL injection vulnerability — user-controlled inputs are interpolated directly into raw SQL query string."
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via f-string query construction",
      "risk": "An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through customer_id or status_filter, enabling data exfiltration, modification, or deletion of the entire database.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query f-string (lines 2-5)",
      "reproduction": "Call the public API with status_filter set to: ' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --  This produces: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = '...' AND status = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --'",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter))",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security: all public-facing endpoints must be injection-safe"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on user-supplied parameters",
      "risk": "Even with parameterized queries, accepting arbitrary strings for status_filter allows nonsensical queries. Missing validation on customer_id format means garbage-in queries hit the database unnecessarily.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — function signature / entry",
      "reproduction": "Pass status_filter='NONEXISTENT_STATUS' or customer_id='' — no error, just silent empty results. Pass customer_id with 10MB string — unbounded input hits DB layer.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id against expected format (e.g., UUID regex). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., PENDING, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED). Reject invalid inputs before query execution.",
      "time_to_fix": "20 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Reliability: endpoint should reject malformed input early"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * returns unbounded columns and rows",
      "risk": "SELECT * exposes all columns including potentially sensitive internal fields (driver notes, internal timestamps, cost). No LIMIT clause means a broad filter could return millions of rows, causing OOM or denial of service on a public endpoint.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SQL query",
      "reproduction": "A customer with 500k deliveries in status DELIVERED hits this endpoint — the entire result set is loaded into memory via fetchall().",
      "fix": "Explicitly list only the columns the API should expose. Add pagination with LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination. Consider streaming results instead of fetchall().",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Performance and security: public endpoints must be bounded and not leak internal schema"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No error handling around database execution",
      "description": "db_conn.execute() can raise connection errors, timeout errors, or operational errors. These will propagate as unhandled exceptions, likely returning a 500 with a stack trace that leaks internal details to the caller.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — db_conn.execute(query)"
    },
    {
      "title": "No authorization check — customer A can query customer B's deliveries",
      "description": "The function trusts whatever customer_id is passed in. There is no verification that the authenticated user is authorized to view deliveries for the requested customer_id. This is an IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — customer_id parameter"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Add logging/metrics for query execution time and result count to detect abuse patterns",
    "Consider adding an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not already exist to support this query pattern",
    "Return a typed dataclass or DTO instead of raw row tuples to decouple the API response from the database schema"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code must NOT be deployed — it is trivially exploitable from the public internet. Any deployment exposes the database to full compromise.",
    "rollback_capability": "If already deployed, rollback is urgent but does not remediate data that may have already been exfiltrated.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for legitimate callers. Adding validation may reject previously-accepted malformed inputs, which is desired behavior.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring — add query timing, error rate, and result-set size metrics before deploying.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["SQL injection allows arbitrary database manipulation including destructive operations", "IDOR allows cross-customer data access"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Immediate rollback if deployed. Audit database logs for injection patterns. Rotate database credentials as a precaution."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 99,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["SQL injection is unambiguous and trivially exploitable", "Lack of input validation and pagination are clearly present", "IDOR vulnerability is evident from the code"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["Cannot verify database schema or existing indexes without access to DB", "Cannot confirm whether authorization is handled in a middleware layer upstream"],
    "assumptions": ["customer_id and status_filter reach this function unsanitized as stated in the prompt", "No ORM or query-builder wrapping db_conn.execute()"]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Reviewing this code for critical issues. Clear SQL injection vulnerability since user input is directly interpolated into the query string.

Classification: `none` — these are well-known code-level fixes (parameterized queries, input validation).

{
  "summary": {

  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via f-string query construction",
      "risk": "An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through customer_id or status_filter, enabling data exfiltration, modification, or deletion of the entire database.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query f-string (lines 2-5)",
      "reproduction": "Call the public API with status_filter set to: ' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --  This produces: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = '...' AND status = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --'",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter))",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security: all public-facing endpoints must be injection-safe"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on user-supplied parameters",
      "risk": "Even with parameterized queries, accepting arbitrary strings for status_filter allows nonsensical queries. Missing validation on customer_id format means garbage-in queries hit the database unnecessarily.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — function signature / entry",
      "reproduction": "Pass status_filter='NONEXISTENT_STATUS' or customer_id='' — no error, just silent empty results. Pass customer_id with 10MB string — unbounded input hits DB layer.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id against expected format (e.g., UUID regex). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., PENDING, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED). Reject invalid inputs before query execution.",
      "time_to_fix": "20 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Reliability: endpoint should reject malformed input early"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * returns unbounded columns and rows",
      "risk": "SELECT * exposes all columns including potentially sensitive internal fields (driver notes, internal timestamps, cost). No LIMIT clause means a broad filter could return millions of rows, causing OOM or denial of service on a public endpoint.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SQL query",
      "reproduction": "A customer with 500k deliveries in status DELIVERED hits this endpoint — the entire result set is loaded into memory via fetchall().",
      "fix": "Explicitly list only the columns the API should expose. Add pagination with LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination. Consider streaming results instead of fetchall().",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Performance and security: public endpoints must be bounded and not leak internal schema"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No error handling around database execution",
      "description": "db_conn.execute() can raise connection errors, timeout errors, or operational errors. These will propagate as unhandled exceptions, likely returning a 500 with a stack trace that leaks internal details to the caller.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — db_conn.execute(query)"
    },
    {
      "title": "No authorization check — customer A can query customer B's deliveries",
      "description": "The function trusts whatever customer_id is passed in. There is no verification that the authenticated user is authorized to view deliveries for the requested customer_id. This is an IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — customer_id parameter"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Add logging/metrics for query execution time and result count to detect abuse patterns",
    "Consider adding an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not already exist to support this query pattern",
    "Return a typed dataclass or DTO instead of raw row tuples to decouple the API response from the database schema"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code must NOT be deployed — it is trivially exploitable from the public internet. Any deployment exposes the database to full compromise.",
    "rollback_capability": "If already deployed, rollback is urgent but does not remediate data that may have already been exfiltrated.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for legitimate callers. Adding validation may reject previously-accepted malformed inputs, which is desired behavior.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring — add query timing, error rate, and result-set size metrics before deploying.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["SQL injection allows arbitrary database manipulation including destructive operations", "IDOR allows cross-customer data access"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Immediate rollback if deployed. Audit database logs for injection patterns. Rotate database credentials as a precaution."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 99,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["SQL injection is unambiguous and trivially exploitable", "Lack of input validation and pagination are clearly present", "IDOR vulnerability is evident from the code"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["Cannot verify database schema or existing indexes without access to DB", "Cannot confirm whether authorization is handled in a middleware layer upstream"],
    "assumptions": ["customer_id and status_filter reach this function unsanitized as stated in the prompt", "No ORM or query-builder wrapping db_conn.execute()"]
  }
}
